pilot_scope.purpose: Prepare a private sandbox discussion for utility-to-enterprise outage intelligence workflows.

items.name: Pilot objective and partner class are defined
items.evidence: The repo frames the prototype around enterprise outage ETA, partner action, auditability, timeout fallback, and closed-loop evaluation.

items.name: Out-of-scope production claims are explicit
items.evidence: Docs state that this is a public-safe prototype and not a production deployment.

items.name: Partner class and synthetic site scope are agreed
items.evidence: Current examples use synthetic partner classes and site prefixes only.

items.name: Pilot contact path and escalation labels are agreed
items.evidence: The prototype stores public-safe contact labels, not private contact details.

items.name: Synthetic data boundary is maintained
items.evidence: Public-safe scan and generated reports use synthetic identifiers only.

items.name: Private authentication material is excluded from public outputs
items.evidence: Reports omit private headers, network targets, and raw operational text.

items.name: Retention and de-identification policy is reviewed before live use
items.evidence: A private implementation needs approved data handling before operational data is used.

items.name: Sandbox flow proves idempotent incident and signal handling
items.evidence: run_partner_sandbox_flow.py covers duplicate event and duplicate field signal behavior.

items.name: Scenario matrix covers core pilot risk cases
items.evidence: run_pilot_scenario_matrix.py covers short outage, prolonged outage, timeout, duplicate, retry, restore, and scope-control cases.

items.name: Readiness gate summarizes go/no-go evidence
items.evidence: generate_readiness_gate.py combines public-safe scan, sandbox flow, scenario matrix, ML benchmark, and shadow evaluation evidence.

items.name: Utility and partner owners are assigned for pilot review
items.evidence: The public repo provides role labels only; named people are intentionally excluded.

items.name: Operator handoff and review cadence are agreed
items.evidence: The runbook proposes executive and operator walkthroughs but does not define a live operating calendar.

items.name: Pilot metrics are defined
items.evidence: Pilot success metrics include ETA error, underestimation rate, timeout fallback rate, delivery attempt rate, audit completeness, and ground-truth coverage.

items.name: Shadow evaluation is benchmark-only
items.evidence: Shadow evaluation compares ETA policies offline and does not change partner-facing decisions.

raci.notes: Confirm private sandbox objectives and non-goals before connecting any private system.
Review incident, ETA revision, timeout, local retry, restoration, and report artifacts.
Approve data minimization, retention, access, and de-identification rules before operational data is used.
Use readiness gate, scenario matrix, pilot report, and risk register as decision evidence.

risk_register.risk: Sandbox authentication is suitable for discussion but not enough for live partner access.
risk_register.impact: A live pilot would require stronger authorization and operational controls.
risk_register.mitigation: Keep this repo local and public-safe; implement production-grade auth in a private service.

risk_register.risk: SQLite and synthetic site prefixes are not a production tenant isolation model.
risk_register.impact: Private pilots need enforced partner-level boundaries and audit review.
risk_register.mitigation: Use managed storage, migration policy, and partner-scoped access checks before live use.

risk_register.risk: Webhook behavior is modeled through a local outbox only.
risk_register.impact: Delivery reliability cannot be certified until a private delivery worker is tested.
risk_register.mitigation: Treat retry evidence as sandbox proof; add private receiver verification before live pilot.
risk_register.production_gate: managed delivery worker and receiver verification

risk_register.risk: ML and shadow evaluation metrics are based on synthetic rows.
risk_register.impact: Metric direction is useful for discussion but not a production performance claim.
risk_register.mitigation: Run shadow evaluation on governed pilot data before changing partner-facing policy.
risk_register.production_gate: approved shadow evaluation on governed data

risk_register.risk: Runbooks are pilot discussion artifacts, not live incident procedures.
risk_register.impact: Operational ownership and escalation paths remain undefined for live use.
risk_register.mitigation: Assign utility and partner owners during private pilot planning.

go_criteria: Synthetic sandbox flow passes from a clean checkout.
Scenario matrix has no failed synthetic scenarios.
Shadow evaluation validates the pilot data contract.
Risk register and production gaps are visible in the pilot pack.
no_go_criteria: Generated reports expose private auth material or network delivery targets.
Sandbox flow cannot prove duplicate handling.
Timeout fallback or restoration closure is missing from evidence.
Governance owners are not assigned before any private data is used.
